beat: Establish the town's mood at dusk in Town Square: fear, fatigue, and rumors of bloodstains without missing bodies.

beat_state.current: Establish the town's mood at dusk in Town Square: fear, fatigue, and rumors of bloodstains without missing bodies.
beat_state.next: Introduce Mitch as the first urgent hook. He asks for help and insists the wizard is involved, but his testimony is inconsistent.

scene.description: The cobbled heart of the harbor town, lit by lanterns and restless conversation. Traders, guards, and dockhands all pass through here, and tonight nearly every conversation circles back to bloodstains, lost hours, and blame.

session_summary: Intro: You arrive in a dusk-lit harbor town gripped by rumors of bloodstains, missing time, and whispered blame toward a reclusive wizard—and the fear is real enough to taste.
session_summary_events: Intro: You arrive in a dusk-lit harbor town gripped by rumors of bloodstains, missing time, and whispered blame toward a reclusive wizard—and the fear is real enough to taste.
story_status: Dusk settles over the harbor town as lanterns flare alive around the cobbles of Town Square. People move with the same exhausted, hollow-eyed rhythm, repeating the same rumors: bloodstains in homes, missing hours, and mornings full of dread with no clear memory of what happened overnight. Some blame the reclusive wizard behind the iron fence, while others whisper that the town itself is cursed. You arrived to verify the rumors, but now you are standing inside them.

history.content: You arrive in a dusk-lit harbor town gripped by rumors of bloodstains, missing time, and whispered blame toward a reclusive wizard—and the fear is real enough to taste.

story.starting_state: Dusk settles over the harbor town as lanterns flare alive around the cobbles of Town Square. People move with the same exhausted, hollow-eyed rhythm, repeating the same rumors: bloodstains in homes, missing hours, and mornings full of dread with no clear memory of what happened overnight. Some blame the reclusive wizard behind the iron fence, while others whisper that the town itself is cursed. You arrived to verify the rumors, but now you are standing inside them.
beat_list: Establish the town's mood at dusk in Town Square: fear, fatigue, and rumors of bloodstains without missing bodies.
Introduce Mitch as the first urgent hook. He asks for help and insists the wizard is involved, but his testimony is inconsistent.
Let the player investigate freely across civic, dockside, temple, and tavern locations to gather concrete clues and witness accounts.
Surface contradictions: missing-time reports, cleaned crime scenes, and records that imply someone is repeatedly resetting events.
Bring the player to a direct confrontation where both Mitch and the wizard can be questioned with evidence in hand.
Reveal the core truth: Mitch commits killings in a dissociative cycle and the wizard has been erasing/repairing memories to prevent wider panic.
Resolve by player choice: side with Mitch, side with the wizard, broker a difficult compromise, or pursue an independent solution.

locations.name: Back Door - Copper Cup
locations.description: A plain wooden door at the rear of the Copper Cup, used by staff and late-night regulars. Crates and empty barrels stack nearby, and the smell of stew drifts out when it opens. A faint dark streak runs along one stone, as if something was once dragged past in a hurry.

locations.name: Bar Counter
locations.description: Shelves of bottles, a till, and crates tucked beneath a polished counter scarred by years of tankards. Mara keeps this vantage point to monitor every table and every rumor that enters the tavern.

locations.name: Copper Cup
locations.description: A low-beamed harbor tavern. Lanterns swing above crowded tables, and the air smells of stew, sea-salt, and woodsmoke. The barkeep Mara watches everything from behind the counter.

locations.name: Docks
locations.description: Wooden piers stretch out over dark water, crowded with fishing boats and the occasional merchant ship. Ropes creak, gulls wheel overhead, and the smell of tar and brine hangs thick in the air.

locations.name: East Alley
locations.description: A narrow alley where scrubbed cobbles never quite lose their stains. Laundry lines sway overhead, shutters crack open when footsteps pass, and several doorframes show marks that look recently cleaned.

locations.name: Fishermen's Shacks
locations.description: A cluster of small wooden shacks perched on pilings above the river. Nets hang to dry, and lantern-light spills from half-open doors late into the night. In more than one doorway, dark stains mar the floorboards with no memory of how they appeared.

locations.name: Harbor Gate
locations.description: An archway of weathered stone opening toward the piers. Guard posts on either side watch the flow of carts and sailors between the town and the docks.

locations.name: Lumberyard
locations.description: Stacks of freshly cut logs and the tang of sap fill the air. A small shack nearby shows scuffed floorboards and a corner where someone scrubbed at dried, dark stains.

locations.name: Market Stalls
locations.description: Canvas-topped stalls ring the edge of the square, half-closed for the evening. Fishmongers rinse boards, spice vendors shutter crates, and everyone trades the same uneasy gossip before heading home.

locations.name: Old Shrine
locations.description: A crumbling wayside shrine tucked into a bend of the alley, its carvings worn by sea-wind and salt. Offerings of shells and coins gather in a shallow stone bowl.

locations.name: Old Well
locations.description: An old stone well at the edge of the square, its rope replaced more than once. Locals say its water never runs dry, and that it connects to forgotten tunnels beneath the town. Faint rust-brown stains cling to some of the stones as though someone once washed something away here.

locations.name: Riverside Path
locations.description: A muddy path following the riverbank toward the outskirts. Reeds whisper in the breeze, and shallow boats are pulled up onto the shore.

locations.name: Smuggler's Entrance
locations.description: A half-concealed grate and narrow stone steps leading below the warehouses. The smell of damp and old river-mud clings to the air here.

locations.name: South Bridge
locations.description: A low stone bridge arching over a narrow river that feeds the harbor. Lanterns line the parapet, and you can see both the town walls and the distant open sea from its center. More than one passerby has reported waking here with aching feet and no memory of how they arrived.

locations.name: Stair Landing
locations.description: A narrow staircase creaks toward the upper rooms. A rope discourages casual wanderers.

locations.name: Storeroom Door
locations.description: A stout oak door with a brass four-dial combination lock. The correct code is 0415.

locations.name: Temple of the Tide
locations.description: A modest stone temple overlooking the square, its doors carved with waves and storm-clouds. Inside, candles flicker before a statue of a calm, watchful sea goddess. Many townsfolk come here seeking answers for the nights they cannot remember.

locations.name: Town Hall
locations.description: A timber-framed civic hall with a cluttered notice board and nervous clerks inside.

locations.name: Town Square
locations.description: The cobbled heart of the harbor town, lit by lanterns and restless conversation. Traders, guards, and dockhands all pass through here, and tonight nearly every conversation circles back to bloodstains, lost hours, and blame.

locations.name: Warehouse Row
locations.description: A line of squat stone warehouses hugs the waterfront. Their heavy doors and barred windows suggest both valuable cargo and things better kept out of sight. One doorframe bears a faded, rusty smear that no one can quite explain.

locations.name: Watch Barracks
locations.description: A sturdy building flying the town's colors. Racks of spears stand by the door, and off-duty guards swap stories on the steps when the weather is mild.

locations.name: Wizard's House
locations.description: A tall stone house with shuttered windows and a heavy iron fence, set just off the square. Lanterns burn late behind the curtains, and a pair of hired guards discourage casual callers.

entities.name: Boatman Jaro
entities.description: Jaro tends a small skiff tied to a worn post. For the right price, he'll ferry people quietly along the river—or look the other way when others do.
memory: Jaro has ferried people along the river after curfew and noticed several passengers unable to recall the trip by morning.
He once found muddy footprints ending at Riverside Path with no matching return trail.

entities.name: Bridge Watcher Sol
entities.description: Sol leans on the bridge railing during most evening watches, counting lanterns and noting who comes and goes along the river path.
memory: Sol logs lantern checks and has noted repeated late-night crossings that no one later remembers making.
He heard shouting near the South Bridge on a storm night, then silence and no witnesses.

entities.name: Brin
entities.description: Mara's husband. A weather-beaten sailor with a limp. Forgetful of his wedding anniversary date.
memory: Brin swears he locked the storeroom himself, but sometimes wakes unsure whether he worked the late shift at all.
He trusts Mara and avoids speaking openly about the stains in front of strangers.

entities.name: Captain Varr
entities.description: Captain Varr is a broad-shouldered veteran with a scar along his jaw. He takes the town's safety personally and has little patience for fools, but respects those who pull their weight.
memory: Varr has increased patrol rotations because reports cluster around the same hours before dawn.
He is suspicious of the wizard, but equally concerned that fear is pushing townsfolk toward vigilantism.

entities.name: Caretaker Ilya
entities.description: Ilya quietly cleans the old shrine and replaces wilted offerings. She claims to have seen strange lights near the well on stormy nights.
memory: Ilya has found fresh offerings at the old shrine placed by hands that later denied visiting.
She believes the town is caught in a cycle that repeats each night with pieces missing each morning.

entities.name: Cleric Serah
entities.description: Serah, a middle-aged cleric of the sea goddess, tends to sailors and townsfolk alike. She listens more than she speaks and keeps a careful eye on omens from the harbor—and on the strange emptiness in the eyes of those who wake up with blood on their doorsteps.
memory: Serah has treated people with stress wounds and torn clothing they cannot explain.
She keeps private notes on recurring symbols linked to the bloodstains and memory lapses.

entities.name: Cook Brenna
entities.description: Brenna is the Copper Cup's harried cook, always juggling pots and shouting orders through the kitchen door. She hears everything that happens near the back rooms.
memory: Brenna hears every argument near the back door and noticed Mitch pacing there before dawn twice this week.
She remembers seeing dark smears on a crate that were gone after sunrise.

entities.name: Deckhand Finn
entities.description: Finn is a young deckhand with rope-burned hands and boundless curiosity. He listens in on sailor gossip and can point out trouble brewing on the piers.
memory: Finn saw someone rinsing a rope at the dock pump well past curfew.
He spreads rumors quickly but gets details right more often than people expect.

entities.name: Dockmaster Hara
entities.description: Hara keeps a ledger tucked under one arm as she strides up and down the docks. She knows which ships arrived late, which cargo went missing, and which captains owe favors. She is quietly furious about the unexplained stains turning up near her warehouses.
memory: Hara tracks cargo and says missing-hour gaps line up with unexplained ledger discrepancies.
She quietly suspects a cover-up larger than simple dockside crime.

entities.name: Edda
entities.description: A scholar cataloging tavern legends, forever scribbling into a leather folio.
memory: Edda is cataloging oral histories and noticed old legends describing memory theft tied to blood oaths.
She thinks the present crisis mirrors a partial account from decades ago.

entities.name: Fence Caris
entities.description: Caris leans in the shadows near a back door, dealing in small stolen goods and no questions asked. She prefers to stay out of sight when guards are near.
memory: Caris sold lock tools to someone matching Mitch's build, but keeps that detail close.
She avoids the Smugglers Entrance on nights when the moon is hidden.

entities.name: Fisher Mira
entities.description: Mira cleans fish with quick, practiced motions, humming an old sea-song under her breath. She keeps an eye on the river for signs of storms or trouble, and she is one of the few who admits she sometimes wakes up with her boots wet and no memory of why.
memory: Mira has awakened with wet boots and river silt in her doorway despite no memory of leaving bed.
She trusts cleric Serah more than the watch to handle delicate truths.

entities.name: Fisher Rian
entities.description: Rian mends nets on an upturned crate outside his shack, grumbling about changing tides and strange catches pulled from deeper waters.
memory: Rian has pulled torn cloth and dark residue from his nets at dawn several times.
He believes someone is moving bodies or evidence by water before sunrise.

entities.name: Fishmonger Talo
entities.description: Talo's stall overflows with the day's catch laid over crushed ice. He knows every captain on the docks and the gossip that rides in with their ships.
memory: Talo hears gossip from every crew and says fear has cut evening market traffic in half.
He remembers seeing Mitch buy extra lamp oil on a night now missing from many memories.

entities.name: Foreman Kesh
entities.description: Kesh oversees the loading crews with a barked order and a sharp eye. He insists everything is above board, but his ledgers don't always match what moves in the night.
memory: Kesh has corrected warehouse counts after nights when workers insist they never unloaded cargo.
He knows Lia uses the tunnels but denies official knowledge.

entities.name: Gate Guard Ren
entities.description: Ren leans on his spear at the harbor gate, sizing up everyone who passes. He's seen enough trouble to be wary, but a kind word or shared drink can loosen his tongue.
memory: Ren has turned away travelers after midnight because too many arrived confused and bloodstained.
He keeps a mental list of townsfolk who wander at night and deny it by morning.

entities.name: Lysa
entities.description: A traveling bard tuning a battered lute, eager for new tales.
memory: Lysa collects songs and rumors and has turned local fear into veiled tavern ballads.
She noticed patrons forgetting songs she just performed the previous evening.

entities.name: Mara
entities.description: The proprietor. Married to Brin. Her wedding anniversary is April 15th. Brisk, perceptive, and inclined to help if treated respectfully.
memory: Mara keeps the storeroom code private and knows someone has tested the lock repeatedly.
She trusts the player more than most outsiders once they show respect for her staff.

entities.name: Mayor Elric
entities.description: Mayor Elric is a tired man in a well-kept coat, juggling merchants' demands and the growing panic over unexplained bloodstains. No one has truly gone missing, but he fears the town is fraying and doesn't know how much longer he can pretend things are under control.
memory: Elric is balancing public calm against private panic and has delayed announcing full incident numbers.
He asked Scribe Loth to archive certain reports until facts can be verified.

entities.name: Mitch
entities.description: A broad-shouldered lumberjack with haunted eyes and a strained need for someone to believe him.
memory: Mitch says he found one of the first bloodstains near the riverside before dawn.
Mitch keeps blaming the wizard, but his story shifts when asked for details.
He wakes exhausted with injuries that suggest violent work he cannot consciously recall.
Mitch says he found one of the first bloodstains near the Riverside Path before dawn.
Mitch keeps blaming the town wizard, but his story changes when pressed for exact times.
Mitch looks exhausted and jittery, like he has not slept properly in several nights.
Mitch's hands are heavily calloused from woodcutting, and he smells like sap, smoke, and wet earth.

entities.name: Novice Arel
entities.description: Arel is a young acolyte sweeping stone floors and lighting candles. Nervous but earnest, they have seen more than Serah realizes and struggle with what to share.
memory: Arel has seen late-night visitors begging Serah for memory blessings.
They found prayer beads near the well after a night of heavy rain and panic bells.

entities.name: Old Tellan
entities.description: Tellan sits on the well's rim spinning stories for anyone who will listen. Some tales are harmless gossip, others hint at things buried under the town and better left alone. Lately his stories keep circling back to nights no one quite remembers.
memory: Tellan claims the old well remembers what people choose to forget.
His stories repeat specific names and dates that later appear in official reports.

entities.name: Player
entities.description: The player character investigating the harbor town mystery.
memory: You came to town chasing rumors about bloodstains and missing memories.

entities.name: Scribe Loth
entities.description: Loth is a meticulous scribe surrounded by inkpots and scrolls. He files complaints, tallies taxes, and occasionally buries inconvenient paperwork for the right favor.
memory: Loth has filed multiple identical complaints from the same households across different dates.
He maintains a hidden duplicate ledger because public records keep getting quietly amended.

entities.name: Smuggler Lia
entities.description: Lia moves quietly through the shadows beneath the warehouses. She trades in information as much as contraband and always seems to know who's asking too many questions.
memory: Lia uses the tunnel routes and has seen signs of hurried cleanup near Warehouse Row before sunrise.
She will trade sensitive information for leverage, not loyalty.

entities.name: Spice Seller Nima
entities.description: Nima sells jars of pungent spices and dried peppers from distant ports. Her keen nose and sharper memory make her a quiet observer of who buys what and why.
memory: Nima tracks unusual purchases and noticed a spike in lye, lamp oil, and bandages.
She remembers voices arguing near Old Well just before dawn on market day.

entities.name: Street Performer Jorin
entities.description: Jorin juggles knives and colored stones for gathered onlookers. Quick with a joke and quicker with his hands, he hears rumors before most anyone else.
memory: Jorin watches faces while performing and can spot who is lying about where they were at night.
He heard Mitch muttering about red hands that do not stay clean.

entities.name: Street Urchin Pip
entities.description: Pip is a sharp-eyed child who knows every shortcut and loose shutter in the alleyways. They trade trinkets and rumors in exchange for coin, food, or kindness.
memory: Pip knows hidden routes between East Alley, the back door, and warehouse drains.
They found a knife wrap with fresh rust near a loose brick and left it where only they can recover it.

entities.name: Thom
entities.description: An off-duty guard who prefers order and dislikes surprises upstairs.
memory: Thom saw watch reports edited after meetings with the mayor and the wizard.
He dislikes surprises and reacts poorly when civilians interfere with barracks protocol.

entities.name: Town Crier Jessa
entities.description: Jessa rings a handbell and repeats the town's latest bad news to anyone who will listen.
memory: Jessa has repeated emergency notices all day and can recite each official statement verbatim.
She noticed the wording of town warnings changed three times within two days.

entities.name: Town Wizard Arlen
entities.description: Arlen is a reclusive wizard who seldom leaves his guarded home. Whispers say he can pluck souls back from the brink and smooth away memories like ink from parchment.
memory: Arlen has used risky memory-working spells to reduce panic after each night of violence.
He believes containing the cycle is safer than exposing every detail to a terrified public.

items.name: Arcane Chalk Fragment
items.description: A chipped stick of silvery chalk used for ward circles and memory-binding sigils.

items.name: Barracks Keyring
items.description: Iron keys for holding lockers, armory racks, and patrol report cabinets.

items.name: Bent Lockpick Set
items.description: A wrapped set of picks missing one fine hook, likely used hard against stubborn locks.

items.name: Bronze Fountain Coin
items.description: A battered bronze coin wedged between the stones of the central fountain.

items.name: Carved Driftwood Charm
items.description: A piece of pale driftwood carved into the shape of a curling wave, hung on a faded blue cord. It smells faintly of incense and sea-salt.

items.name: Cellar Brass Ring
items.description: A brass keyring with one polished key worn smooth from daily use at the Copper Cup storeroom door.

items.name: Cracked Spyglass
items.description: A once-fine brass spyglass with a hairline crack across one lens. It smells faintly of salt and oil and bears tiny etchings matching the town's crest.

items.name: Frayed Mooring Rope
items.description: A length of mooring rope stained a darker color near one end. Up close, the fibers look as though they were cut more than worn through by the sea.

items.name: Guard's Lost Signet
items.description: A small silver signet ring bearing the town's crest, scuffed as though it has been dragged along stone. It looks recently dropped.

items.name: Hidden Alley Knife
items.description: A narrow-bladed knife tucked behind a loose brick at knee height. Its handle is wrapped in worn blue cloth, and a faint rust stain darkens the edge.

items.name: Hidden Scrap
items.description: A grease-stained note tucked behind a loose crate: 'cellar restock / upstairs lock is our wedding anniversary date'.

items.name: Night Patrol Logbook
items.description: A worn ledger listing patrol routes with several entries crossed out and rewritten.

items.name: Salt-Stained Prayer Beads
items.description: A loop of smooth wooden beads, crusted with a thin line of salt where they rest against wet robes. Some of the beads are carved with tiny wave symbols.

items.name: Shattered Lantern Glass
items.description: A pouch of carefully collected lantern shards from the bridge, some edges marked with dried reddish residue.

items.name: Silver Memory Vial
items.description: A stoppered silver vial etched with runes designed to hold a single memory imprint for later restoration.

items.name: Smugglers' Ledger
items.description: A slim ledger bound in cracked leather, its pages filled with coded notes about shipments, routes, and initials. A few entries are smudged with what looks like river-mud.

items.name: Wax-Sealed Incident Packet
items.description: A tied packet of witness statements and guard notes marked for the mayors desk.

items.name: Weathered Notice
items.description: A curling parchment describing strange lights and disturbances near the old well.

items.name: Woodcutter's Axe
items.description: A heavy ash-wood axe with a nicked steel head. The haft has fresh grip marks and old dark staining near the pommel.

nodes.description: A plain wooden door at the rear of the Copper Cup, used by staff and late-night regulars. Crates and empty barrels stack nearby, and the smell of stew drifts out when it opens. A faint dark streak runs along one stone, as if something was once dragged past in a hurry.
Shelves of bottles, a till, and crates tucked beneath a polished counter scarred by years of tankards. Mara keeps this vantage point to monitor every table and every rumor that enters the tavern.
A low-beamed harbor tavern. Lanterns swing above crowded tables, and the air smells of stew, sea-salt, and woodsmoke. The barkeep Mara watches everything from behind the counter.
Wooden piers stretch out over dark water, crowded with fishing boats and the occasional merchant ship. Ropes creak, gulls wheel overhead, and the smell of tar and brine hangs thick in the air.

A narrow alley where scrubbed cobbles never quite lose their stains. Laundry lines sway overhead, shutters crack open when footsteps pass, and several doorframes show marks that look recently cleaned.

A cluster of small wooden shacks perched on pilings above the river. Nets hang to dry, and lantern-light spills from half-open doors late into the night. In more than one doorway, dark stains mar the floorboards with no memory of how they appeared.
An archway of weathered stone opening toward the piers. Guard posts on either side watch the flow of carts and sailors between the town and the docks.
Stacks of freshly cut logs and the tang of sap fill the air. A small shack nearby shows scuffed floorboards and a corner where someone scrubbed at dried, dark stains.
Canvas-topped stalls ring the edge of the square, half-closed for the evening. Fishmongers rinse boards, spice vendors shutter crates, and everyone trades the same uneasy gossip before heading home.
A crumbling wayside shrine tucked into a bend of the alley, its carvings worn by sea-wind and salt. Offerings of shells and coins gather in a shallow stone bowl.

An old stone well at the edge of the square, its rope replaced more than once. Locals say its water never runs dry, and that it connects to forgotten tunnels beneath the town. Faint rust-brown stains cling to some of the stones as though someone once washed something away here.
A muddy path following the riverbank toward the outskirts. Reeds whisper in the breeze, and shallow boats are pulled up onto the shore.
A half-concealed grate and narrow stone steps leading below the warehouses. The smell of damp and old river-mud clings to the air here.

A low stone bridge arching over a narrow river that feeds the harbor. Lanterns line the parapet, and you can see both the town walls and the distant open sea from its center. More than one passerby has reported waking here with aching feet and no memory of how they arrived.
A narrow staircase creaks toward the upper rooms. A rope discourages casual wanderers.
A stout oak door with a brass four-dial combination lock. The correct code is 0415.

A modest stone temple overlooking the square, its doors carved with waves and storm-clouds. Inside, candles flicker before a statue of a calm, watchful sea goddess. Many townsfolk come here seeking answers for the nights they cannot remember.
A timber-framed civic hall with a cluttered notice board and nervous clerks inside.

The cobbled heart of the harbor town, lit by lanterns and restless conversation. Traders, guards, and dockhands all pass through here, and tonight nearly every conversation circles back to bloodstains, lost hours, and blame.

A line of squat stone warehouses hugs the waterfront. Their heavy doors and barred windows suggest both valuable cargo and things better kept out of sight. One doorframe bears a faded, rusty smear that no one can quite explain.
A sturdy building flying the town's colors. Racks of spears stand by the door, and off-duty guards swap stories on the steps when the weather is mild.
A tall stone house with shuttered windows and a heavy iron fence, set just off the square. Lanterns burn late behind the curtains, and a pair of hired guards discourage casual callers.
Jaro tends a small skiff tied to a worn post. For the right price, he'll ferry people quietly along the river—or look the other way when others do.
Sol leans on the bridge railing during most evening watches, counting lanterns and noting who comes and goes along the river path.
Mara's husband. A weather-beaten sailor with a limp. Forgetful of his wedding anniversary date.
Captain Varr is a broad-shouldered veteran with a scar along his jaw. He takes the town's safety personally and has little patience for fools, but respects those who pull their weight.
Ilya quietly cleans the old shrine and replaces wilted offerings. She claims to have seen strange lights near the well on stormy nights.

Serah, a middle-aged cleric of the sea goddess, tends to sailors and townsfolk alike. She listens more than she speaks and keeps a careful eye on omens from the harbor—and on the strange emptiness in the eyes of those who wake up with blood on their doorsteps.
Brenna is the Copper Cup's harried cook, always juggling pots and shouting orders through the kitchen door. She hears everything that happens near the back rooms.
Finn is a young deckhand with rope-burned hands and boundless curiosity. He listens in on sailor gossip and can point out trouble brewing on the piers.

Hara keeps a ledger tucked under one arm as she strides up and down the docks. She knows which ships arrived late, which cargo went missing, and which captains owe favors. She is quietly furious about the unexplained stains turning up near her warehouses.
A scholar cataloging tavern legends, forever scribbling into a leather folio.
Caris leans in the shadows near a back door, dealing in small stolen goods and no questions asked. She prefers to stay out of sight when guards are near.

Mira cleans fish with quick, practiced motions, humming an old sea-song under her breath. She keeps an eye on the river for signs of storms or trouble, and she is one of the few who admits she sometimes wakes up with her boots wet and no memory of why.
Rian mends nets on an upturned crate outside his shack, grumbling about changing tides and strange catches pulled from deeper waters.
Talo's stall overflows with the day's catch laid over crushed ice. He knows every captain on the docks and the gossip that rides in with their ships.
Kesh oversees the loading crews with a barked order and a sharp eye. He insists everything is above board, but his ledgers don't always match what moves in the night.
Ren leans on his spear at the harbor gate, sizing up everyone who passes. He's seen enough trouble to be wary, but a kind word or shared drink can loosen his tongue.
A traveling bard tuning a battered lute, eager for new tales.
The proprietor. Married to Brin. Her wedding anniversary is April 15th. Brisk, perceptive, and inclined to help if treated respectfully.

Mayor Elric is a tired man in a well-kept coat, juggling merchants' demands and the growing panic over unexplained bloodstains. No one has truly gone missing, but he fears the town is fraying and doesn't know how much longer he can pretend things are under control.
A broad-shouldered lumberjack with haunted eyes and a strained need for someone to believe him.
Arel is a young acolyte sweeping stone floors and lighting candles. Nervous but earnest, they have seen more than Serah realizes and struggle with what to share.

Tellan sits on the well's rim spinning stories for anyone who will listen. Some tales are harmless gossip, others hint at things buried under the town and better left alone. Lately his stories keep circling back to nights no one quite remembers.
The player character investigating the harbor town mystery.
Loth is a meticulous scribe surrounded by inkpots and scrolls. He files complaints, tallies taxes, and occasionally buries inconvenient paperwork for the right favor.
Lia moves quietly through the shadows beneath the warehouses. She trades in information as much as contraband and always seems to know who's asking too many questions.
Nima sells jars of pungent spices and dried peppers from distant ports. Her keen nose and sharper memory make her a quiet observer of who buys what and why.
Jorin juggles knives and colored stones for gathered onlookers. Quick with a joke and quicker with his hands, he hears rumors before most anyone else.
Pip is a sharp-eyed child who knows every shortcut and loose shutter in the alleyways. They trade trinkets and rumors in exchange for coin, food, or kindness.
An off-duty guard who prefers order and dislikes surprises upstairs.
Jessa rings a handbell and repeats the town's latest bad news to anyone who will listen.
Arlen is a reclusive wizard who seldom leaves his guarded home. Whispers say he can pluck souls back from the brink and smooth away memories like ink from parchment.
A chipped stick of silvery chalk used for ward circles and memory-binding sigils.
Iron keys for holding lockers, armory racks, and patrol report cabinets.
A wrapped set of picks missing one fine hook, likely used hard against stubborn locks.
A battered bronze coin wedged between the stones of the central fountain.
A piece of pale driftwood carved into the shape of a curling wave, hung on a faded blue cord. It smells faintly of incense and sea-salt.
A brass keyring with one polished key worn smooth from daily use at the Copper Cup storeroom door.
A once-fine brass spyglass with a hairline crack across one lens. It smells faintly of salt and oil and bears tiny etchings matching the town's crest.
A length of mooring rope stained a darker color near one end. Up close, the fibers look as though they were cut more than worn through by the sea.
A small silver signet ring bearing the town's crest, scuffed as though it has been dragged along stone. It looks recently dropped.
A narrow-bladed knife tucked behind a loose brick at knee height. Its handle is wrapped in worn blue cloth, and a faint rust stain darkens the edge.
A grease-stained note tucked behind a loose crate: 'cellar restock / upstairs lock is our wedding anniversary date'.
A worn ledger listing patrol routes with several entries crossed out and rewritten.
A loop of smooth wooden beads, crusted with a thin line of salt where they rest against wet robes. Some of the beads are carved with tiny wave symbols.
A pouch of carefully collected lantern shards from the bridge, some edges marked with dried reddish residue.
A stoppered silver vial etched with runes designed to hold a single memory imprint for later restoration.
A slim ledger bound in cracked leather, its pages filled with coded notes about shipments, routes, and initials. A few entries are smudged with what looks like river-mud.
A tied packet of witness statements and guard notes marked for the mayors desk.
A curling parchment describing strange lights and disturbances near the old well.
A heavy ash-wood axe with a nicked steel head. The haft has fresh grip marks and old dark staining near the pommel.
